name: Mountain Star
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving El Paso and Southern New Mexico since 1935, offering checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, and loan products with low minimum deposits.
description_long:
Mountain Star Federal Credit Union was founded in 1935 by El Paso Electric Company employees and has operated as a member-owned financial institution for nearly 90 years. The credit union is deeply rooted in the El Paso and Southern New Mexico communities, positioning itself as a locally-invested alternative to traditional banks with a commitment to member financial well-being.

Mountain Star offers a comprehensive suite of deposit accounts including share accounts (savings), share draft accounts (checking), money market accounts, IRAs, CDs with terms ranging from 6 to 36 months, and specialty savings accounts like Holiday Club and Vacation Club. Loan products include home loans and auto loans, with an online application process available. The institution also provides additional services such as check ordering, insurance products through truSTAGE, shared branching access to other credit union locations, and a mobile app available on both Android and Apple platforms.

The credit union distinguishes itself through exceptionally low minimum deposit requirements—as little as $5 for youth accounts and $10 for most regular accounts—making membership accessible to consumers with limited savings. Their 6-month CDs offer a competitive 2.53% APY, and the institution emphasizes community involvement and financial education through their newly launched Financial Education page. As a federally-chartered credit union (NMLS ID #1490205), they provide the safety of deposit insurance and member-focused governance.

Mountain Star is best suited for local El Paso and Southern New Mexico residents seeking basic to intermediate banking services with minimal barriers to entry and a community-focused approach. However, potential members should note that CD rates, while competitive for shorter terms, decline significantly for longer-term products (24-36 month CDs at 2.53-3.05% APY), and the website provides limited detail on loan terms, rates, or approval processes.
services: Share accounts (savings accounts) with $10 minimum deposit
Share draft accounts (checking accounts) with $1,000 minimum balance
Money market accounts with tiered rates based on balance ($2,500+)
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) in 6, 12, 24, and 36-month terms
Specialty savings accounts (Holiday Club, Vacation Club, Rainy Day Account, Star Saver)
Shared branching network access to other credit union locations
truSTAGE insurance products and life insurance
pros: Exceptionally low minimum deposits—as little as $5 for youth accounts and $10 for most account types, making membership highly accessible
Competitive CD rates on shorter terms—6-month CDs at 2.53% APY and 12-month CDs at 3.82% APY
90-year history and deep community roots in El Paso and Southern New Mexico since 1935
Member-owned structure ensures profits benefit members rather than external shareholders
Shared branching network allowing access to other credit union locations
Multiple savings account options including specialty accounts like Vacation Club and Holiday Club
Free financial education resources and community event participation opportunities
Mobile app available on both Android and Apple platforms for account management
cons: CD rates decline significantly for longer terms—24 and 36-month CDs drop to 2.53-3.05% APY, making them uncompetitive versus shorter-term options
Website provides minimal information on auto and home loan terms, rates, approval timelines, or eligibility requirements
Limited geographic availability—primarily serves El Paso and Southern New Mexico; membership may be restricted to those with workplace affiliation or community ties
Savings account APYs are very low across most products (0.10-0.30%), offering minimal returns on deposits
No mention of overdraft protection, credit card products, or other consumer lending options commonly offered by larger credit unions
best_for: El Paso and Southern New Mexico residents seeking basic checking and savings accounts with low barriers to membership
Savers with 6-12 month CD terms looking for competitive rates on shorter-duration certificates
Young people and students who qualify for youth accounts with minimal deposit requirements
Members of El Paso Electric Company or those with eligibility based on community or employer affiliation
diagnosis: Mountain Star is best for local El Paso and Southern New Mexico residents seeking straightforward banking services with minimal account minimums and community-focused values, particularly those eligible through employer or community ties. The main caveat is that savings rates are very low across most products, longer-term CD rates are uncompetitive, and the institution provides limited transparency on loan products and terms, requiring direct contact for specific details.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Mountain Star — Credit Union in El Paso, TX | CreditDoc
meta_description: Mountain Star — NCUA-insured credit union in El Paso, TX. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
